question: Does the response contain exactly 4 distinct storyboard panels/shots as requested?
Does each of the 4 shots include both a generated image and accompanying text in an interleaved format?
Are the 4 storyboard panels presented in the correct narrative sequence: Subway → Button Press → Peeling Effect → Mountain Peak?
Is each generated image placed immediately adjacent to (or directly above) its corresponding textual annotations?
Are all generated images rendered in a black-and-white line art style as specified?
In every image where headphones appear, is the bright yellow color applied ONLY to the headphones while the rest of the image remains black-and-white?
Does the image for Shot 1 clearly depict a crowded, noisy morning rush-hour subway environment?
Does the image for Shot 2 show a close-up of a hand specifically pressing a button on the headphones?
Does the image for Shot 3 visually represent the abstract effect of the environment peeling away like shattered fragments?
Does the image for Shot 4 clearly depict a vast, empty snow-capped mountain peak?
Does the text for each of the 4 shots explicitly include cinematographic annotations (e.g., shot size, camera movement)?
Does the text for each of the 4 shots explicitly include sound effect descriptions?
Do the cinematographic annotations utilize professional film terminology (e.g., Wide Shot, Close-up, Pan, Dolly) rather than just casual descriptions?
Does the sound effect description for Shot 1 reference ambient noise, crowd chatter, or subway sounds?
Does the sound effect description for Shot 2 reference a 'click', button press, or activation sound?
Does the sound effect description for Shot 3 reference shattering, cracking, peeling, or whooshing sounds?
Does the sound effect description for Shot 4 convey silence, wind, or serenity to contrast with the opening shot?
Does the text annotation for Shot 2 correctly identify the shot size as a Close-Up (CU) or Extreme Close-Up (ECU)?
Is the visual style (line thickness, shading technique) consistent across all 4 generated images?
Do the headphones maintain a consistent design across all images where they are visible?
Is the text formatted in a clear, professional manner (e.g., using headers or labels like 'Shot 1:', 'SFX:')?
Does the overall storyboard effectively communicate the 'One-Click Switch to a Parallel Universe' theme through the progression of the 4 shots?
